text: The morning field remembers every footstep.
Light rests on the hills as if listening.
A quiet wind rearranges the day.
Water keeps the shape of every passing cloud.
The forest speaks in patient syllables.
Rain writes its own brief history.
The horizon softens when the heart does.
A single leaf can steady the whole afternoon.
The sky leans closer in moments of clarity.
Stillness is the oldest form of weather.
